brief_document.title: Two-phase activity dispatch with async completion via MailboxSender
brief_document.purpose: Replace the blocking dirty-NIF activity dispatch with a two-phase normal-NIF dispatch plus BEAM-receive await, eliminating dirty scheduler thread consumption during long-running activities and enabling hundreds of concurrent activities per engine.
brief_document.task: Split run_activity/3 into dispatch_activity/3 (normal NIF) and await_activity_result/1 (BEAM selective receive). dispatch_activity records ActivityScheduled/Started, clones a MailboxSender for the caller PID, spawns a Tokio fire-and-forget task that calls ActivityDispatcher and delivers the result via MailboxSender::send. await_activity_result waits for the completion message and records ActivityCompleted/ActivityFailed. Update the ActivityDispatcher trait to an async fire-and-forget model. Update the aion_flow Gleam SDK to use the two-phase pattern. Ensure the Resolver handles both phases on replay.

requirements.title: dispatch_activity normal NIF
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL register dispatch_activity/3 as a normal (non-dirty) NIF that records ActivityScheduled and ActivityStarted events through the Recorder, obtains a MailboxSender for the calling BEAM process, spawns a Tokio task via Handle::spawn that calls the ActivityDispatcher and delivers the result via MailboxSender::send, and returns a correlation ID binary to the calling process.
acceptance: dispatch_activity/3 is registered as NifEntry::new (not dirty).
ActivityScheduled and ActivityStarted events are recorded in the event store on dispatch.
The Tokio task holds a cloned MailboxSender (Clone+Send+Sync) captured at dispatch time.
The NIF returns within microseconds — no blocking on activity execution.
On replay, the Resolver returns the recorded correlation ID without spawning a Tokio task.

requirements.title: Tokio completion task with MailboxSender delivery
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL spawn a Tokio task from the dispatch NIF that calls ActivityDispatcher::dispatch, constructs a completion message tuple {activity_complete, CorrelationId, ResultBinary}, and delivers it to the workflow process via MailboxSender::send. On activity failure, the message SHALL be {activity_failed, CorrelationId, ErrorBinary}.
acceptance: The Tokio task is spawned via tokio::runtime::Handle::spawn (fire-and-forget from the NIF).
MailboxSender::send is called from the Tokio worker thread with a structured Term tuple.
Activity success delivers {activity_complete, correlation_id, result_payload}.
Activity failure delivers {activity_failed, correlation_id, error_payload}.
The spawned task does not hold any BEAM scheduler resources.

requirements.title: await_activity_result BEAM receive
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL implement await_activity_result/1 as a NIF that performs a BEAM selective receive on the correlation ID, wakes when the completion message arrives, records ActivityCompleted or ActivityFailed through the Recorder, and returns the result to the calling Gleam code.
acceptance: await_activity_result/1 blocks the BEAM process in a selective receive (zero NIF threads consumed during wait).
On receiving {activity_complete, id, result}, records ActivityCompleted and returns {ok, result}.
On receiving {activity_failed, id, error}, records ActivityFailed and returns {error, reason}.
On replay, the Resolver returns the cached ActivityCompleted/ActivityFailed result immediately without entering a receive.

requirements.title: ActivityDispatcher trait async adaptation
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL update the ActivityDispatcher trait from synchronous dispatch(&self, name, input, config) -> Result<String, String> to an async model compatible with fire-and-forget Tokio task execution. The trait SHALL support both the new async path and maintain backward compatibility for the existing server dispatcher.
acceptance: ActivityDispatcher can be called from a Tokio task without blocking a BEAM thread.
The existing server-side dispatcher (gRPC worker dispatch) works with the new trait shape.
Test dispatchers can be constructed without async runtime setup.

requirements.title: aion_flow Gleam SDK two-phase activity call
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL update the aion_flow Gleam SDK activity.run() to use the two-phase pattern: call dispatch_activity/3 to get a correlation ID, then call await_activity_result/1 to get the result. The SDK surface SHALL remain unchanged — activity.run() still returns the activity result directly.
acceptance: aion_flow activity.run() internally calls dispatch_activity then await_activity_result.
The public API of activity.run() is unchanged — callers see no difference.
The @external declarations in aion/internal/ffi.gleam are updated for the new NIF names.
gleam build succeeds for aion_flow and examples/hello-world.

requirements.title: Remove blocking run_activity NIF
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL remove the old blocking run_activity/3 NIF entry and its implementation once the two-phase dispatch is wired end-to-end.
acceptance: run_activity/3 is no longer registered in engine_nif_entries().
nif_activity.rs blocking dispatch path is removed.
No dirty NIF entries remain for activity dispatch.
All tests pass with the new dispatch model.

boundaries: SHALL split run_activity into dispatch_activity (normal NIF) and await_activity_result (BEAM receive).
SHALL use MailboxSender::send from Tokio worker threads for completion delivery.
SHALL record the same event sequence: ActivityScheduled, ActivityStarted, ActivityCompleted/Failed.
SHALL ensure Resolver handles both phases on replay without spawning Tokio tasks.
SHALL NOT change the aion_flow public SDK surface — activity.run() signature stays the same.
SHALL NOT introduce dirty NIF usage for dispatch — the dispatch phase is a normal NIF.
SHALL NOT require any beamr changes — all primitives (MailboxSender, Handle::spawn) already exist.
SHALL NOT change the event schema or EventStore contract.
verification: dispatch_activity NIF is registered as normal (not dirty) in engine_nif_entries().
No dirty NIF entries exist for activity dispatch after migration.
Integration test: dispatch + await round-trips correctly with a test dispatcher.
Replay test: recorded history replays both phases without live dispatch.
gleam build succeeds for aion_flow and examples/hello-world.

constraints.text: unsafe_code = deny. No unsafe in the crate.
No #[allow]/#[expect]/#[ignore] lint bypasses per CLAUDE.md; tests needing a runtime gate it at runtime, never #[ignore].
lib.rs/mod.rs are declarations and re-exports only.
aion depends only on aion-core, aion-store, aion-package, and beamr among workspace crates — no aion-server, no store backend crate, no networking crate. Structural; must hold.
Transport-agnostic: no HTTP, gRPC, or WebSocket dependency or code anywhere in the crate.
Only the runtime module imports beamr; every other module uses the RuntimeHandle (per D1).
All library errors are thiserror EngineError variants; no anyhow in this library crate. No .unwrap()/.expect() outside tests.
Mutex/RwLock poison handled explicitly and mapped to a typed EngineError; no .unwrap() on a lock guard.
WorkflowStatus is read from aion-core's event projection; the registry's cached status is never the source of truth (per aion-core CO7).
aion hardcodes no scheduler thread count, no timeout, and no retry default; configurable values come from the builder or are deferred to beamr's own default.
Module namespacing uses aion-package's transform unchanged; the engine never re-derives namespaced names (per D8).
Engine tests run against InMemoryStore; no test requires a database (per aion-core D8).

resolved_context.intention: Build the heart of the engine — the aion crate you embed. Given an EventStore, a set of loaded .aion workflow packages, and registered NIFs, EngineBuilder produces a live Engine that starts workflows, runs each as a BEAM process on an embedded beamr runtime, supervises them through a three-level tree, dispatches their activities as linked child processes, and cancels or reports on them by ID. Starting a workflow is spawning a process; cancelling it is killing that process, which through links kills its activity children too; an activity crash propagates an exit signal the trapping workflow process receives as a message. The crate is transport-agnostic — no HTTP/gRPC/WebSocket — keeping the embedded path free of network dependencies. This cluster owns lifecycle, process management, supervision, and module loading; it defers durability/replay (AD) and time/signals/queries/children/concurrency (AT) to their owners, surfacing their entry points on Engine and stating the seams explicitly.

provenance.quote: Build the heart of the engine — the aion crate you embed. Given an EventStore, a set of loaded .aion workflow packages, and registered NIFs, EngineBuilder produces a live Engine that starts workflows,
